response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished senior/staff software engineer with 14+ years of experience in web, mobile, and backend systems, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep hardware expertise — SoC and IP modeling, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD analysis, and AI accelerator optimization — none of which appear in his resume. His AI-related work is limited to software-level agent tooling (MCP frameworks), not hardware-software co-design or deep-learning workload performance at the silicon level. There is no evidence of the chip architecture, system modeling, or DNN inference optimization background this role requires.
strengths: 14+ years of software engineering experience satisfies the BS+8yr education/experience threshold on paper
Demonstrated low-latency, high-throughput systems work at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP req/s, async I/O, fiber-based concurrency in DLang)
Some exposure to embedded systems and hardware-adjacent work (robotic arms, custom test infrastructure) shows breadth beyond pure web development
concerns: Zero SoC or IP modeling experience — the core technical requirement of the role is entirely absent from the resume
No GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or AI accelerator background; AI work is limited to high-level agent software, not deep-learning workload performance or hardware co-design
Career trajectory is firmly in web/fullstack/backend software; there is no signal of a transition toward or interest in hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience
No AI accelerator or GPU workload expertise
